title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Create isolated environment with numerical computing libraries for CPU-only MCMC experiments

steps.step1: Create virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install core dependencies: pip install numpy scipy numba matplotlib seaborn pandas
steps.step3: Install statistical packages: pip install statsmodels scikit-learn
steps.step4: Verify Numba JIT compilation works: create test script with @njit decorator
steps.step5: Create directory structure: mkdir -p src/ data/ results/ figures/ logs/
steps.step6: Set random seed default: RANDOM_SEED_BASE = 42 for all experiments

title: Generate MCMC Problem Instances
description: Generate standardized problem instances matching DISCS benchmark specifications for direct comparability

steps.step1: Lattice Ising Models (same as DISCS): 2D grid with periodic boundary conditions
steps.step2: Generate Lattice Ising: sizes [100, 400, 900] variables (10x10, 20x20, 30x30)
steps.step3: Coupling strengths J ∈ {0.2, 0.44, 0.6} representing: below/above/at critical (Tc)
steps.step4: Random Ising Models: sizes [100, 400] variables, couplings J_ij ~ N(0, J^2)
steps.step5: Frustrated couplings: 50% negative interactions for glassy landscapes
steps.step6: Discrete Gaussian Mixtures: 2-10 modes, 50-200 dimensions, ground truth available
steps.step7: Bernoulli models for theory validation: independent p_i ~ Uniform(0.3, 0.7)
steps.step8: Save all instances to data/problems/ with JSON metadata including ground truth where available
steps.step9: Verify problem generation: check energy landscapes have expected properties

title: Implement Fixed-Step GWG Baseline
description: Implement standard Gibbs-With-Gradients with fixed step sizes as simple baseline

steps.step1: Implement GWG sampler with locally-balanced Barker proposal: π_i = sqrt(t_i)/(1+sqrt(t_i))
steps.step2: Implement gradient computation via finite differences: ∂log p(x)/∂x_i
steps.step3: Test on 10D Bernoulli with known ground truth to verify correctness
steps.step4: Fixed step sizes to test: σ ∈ {0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5}
steps.step5: Run 5 seeds per step size on 100D Lattice Ising (J=0.2, below critical)
steps.step6: Measure: ESS (Effective Sample Size), acceptance rate, jump distance per iteration

title: Implement Grid-Search Heuristic Baseline
description: Implement simple adaptive heuristic: short pilot chains, select best, then freeze

steps.step1: PARALLEL: Run 5 pilot chains in parallel with each σ ∈ {0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5}
steps.step2: Selection criterion: Choose σ with highest empirical jump distance
steps.step3: Production phase: Freeze selected σ, run full chain for 10000 iterations
steps.step4: Compare total cost: pilot + production vs. pure adaptive methods
steps.step5: Run 10 seeds on: 100D/400D Lattice Ising, 100D Random Ising
steps.step6: Output: grid_search_results.json with pilot selection and final performance
steps.step7: Analysis: Does simple heuristic match complex online adaptation?

title: Implement ALBP (Acceptance-Rate Targeting)
description: Implement Adaptive LBP from Sun et al. 2022 with stochastic approximation

steps.step1: Implement Robbins-Monro update: R_{t+1} ← R_t + η_t(a_t - δ)
steps.step2: Target acceptance rate: δ = 0.574 (optimal for locally-balanced)
steps.step3: Learning rate schedule: η_t = η_0 / (1 + t/τ) with η_0 = 0.1, τ = 1000
steps.step4: Map R to step size: σ = exp(R) to ensure positivity
steps.step5: Burn-in adaptation: Track R trajectory to assess convergence
steps.step6: Test on 100D Bernoulli: verify acceptance rate converges to ~0.574
steps.step7: Validate against Sun et al. 2022 Figure 5: reproduce within ±0.05

title: Implement AB-Sampler (Jump-Distance Maximization)
description: Implement Any-Scale Balanced sampler from Sun et al. 2023a

steps.step1: Implement empirical jump distance: J(σ) = E[||x' - x||] averaged over window
steps.step2: Adaptation: Maximize J(σ) via quadratic approximation with Gaussian integral
steps.step3: Joint adaptation: Tune both step size σ and balancing α if applicable
steps.step4: Window size: W = 100 iterations for empirical jump distance estimation
steps.step5: Adaptation frequency: Update every W iterations
steps.step6: Test on 100D Gaussian: verify σ converges to near-optimal value
steps.step7: Measure stability: Track variance of σ estimates during adaptation

title: Implement ACS (Cyclical Scheduling)
description: Implement Automatic Cyclical Scheduling from Pynadath et al. 2024

steps.step1: Implement cyclical step size: σ(t) = σ_min + 0.5(σ_max - σ_min)(1 + cos(πT_cur/T_i))
steps.step2: Cycle length: T_i = 1000 iterations
steps.step3: Automatic tuning: Adjust σ_min, σ_max based on acceptance rate targeting ρ* = 0.5
steps.step4: Acceptance rate tracking: Moving average over last 100 proposals
steps.step5: Test on multimodal target: verify escapes from local modes
steps.step6: Compare against fixed-target methods on same multimodal problems

title: Phase 1: Pilot Validation
description: Validate implementations on tractable problems before large-scale experiments

steps.step1: Bernoulli model (100D independent): Ground truth ESS computable analytically
steps.step2: Reproduce Sun et al. 2022: Vary acceptance rate, measure ESS, find optimum
steps.step3: Success: Optimal rate within ±0.05 of 0.574 (theoretical prediction)
steps.step4: Gaussian model (100D): Compare ALBP vs AB-sampler vs ground truth
steps.step5: Measure adaptation convergence: iterations until parameters stabilize
steps.step6: Validate ESS computation: Compare our ESS estimator against known analytical form
steps.step7: Output: pilot_validation/ with convergence plots and statistical checks
steps.step8: Decision gate: If pilot fails, diagnose and fix before proceeding

title: Phase 2: Head-to-Head Comparison
description: Systematic comparison of all adaptive methods on standardized problems

steps.step1: Problems: Lattice Ising 100D/400D, Random Ising 100D/400D (same as DISCS)
steps.step2: Temperatures: Below (J=0.2), at (J=0.44), above (J=0.6) critical
steps.step3: Methods: ALBP, AB-sampler, ACS, Fixed-step GWG (σ=0.1), Grid-search
steps.step4: Seeds: 10 random seeds per (problem, method, temperature) - PARALLEL across 2 cores
steps.step5: Chain length: 5000 iterations (2000 warmup, 3000 sampling)
steps.step6: Metrics per run: ESS (via AR(1) spectral method), ESS/sec, acceptance rate, jump distance
steps.step7: Wall-clock timing: Python time.perf_counter() for fair comparison
steps.step8: Parallelization: Run different seeds concurrently using multiprocessing.Pool(2)
steps.step9: Output: phase2_results.json with all raw measurements

title: Statistical Rigor: Effect Sizes and Power Analysis
description: Apply rigorous statistical analysis to compare methods with meaningful differences

steps.step1: Effect size: Compute Cohen's d for ESS differences between each method pair
steps.step2: Interpretation: d < 0.2 (negligible), 0.2-0.5 (small), 0.5-0.8 (medium), >0.8 (large)
steps.step3: Meaningful difference threshold: d > 0.5 (medium effect) for claiming superiority
steps.step4: Mann-Whitney U test: Non-parametric comparison of ESS distributions
steps.step5: Bonferroni correction: α_adjusted = 0.05 / (number of comparisons)
steps.step6: Power analysis: With n=10 seeds, verify 80% power to detect d=0.5 at α=0.05
steps.step7: Confidence intervals: 95% CI for mean ESS differences
steps.step8: Output: statistical_analysis.json with effect sizes, p-values, CIs

title: Phase 3a: Phase Transition Stress Test
description: Characterize failure modes at critical temperature where theory breaks down

steps.step1: Critical temperature for 2D Ising: J_c ≈ 0.44 (theoretical)
steps.step2: Test lattice sizes: 100D, 400D at J = 0.44
steps.step3: Measure: Acceptance rate trajectory over adaptation
steps.step4: Measure: ESS degradation vs off-critical (J=0.2, J=0.6)
steps.step5: Measure: Gradient variance (indicator of critical slowing down)
steps.step6: Compare: ALBP (may deviate from 0.574) vs AB-sampler (may be unstable)
steps.step7: Seeds: 10 seeds per configuration - PARALLEL across 2 cores
steps.step8: Expected: >50% performance degradation validates Roberts & Rosenthal warning

title: Phase 3b: Pre-Convergence Adaptation Stability
description: Test adaptation stability during burn-in before chain reaches stationarity

steps.step1: Initialization strategies: (a) All zeros, (b) Random, (c) High-energy (far from mode)
steps.step2: Track: Parameter trajectories during first 3000 iterations
steps.step3: Measure: Variance of parameters in early phase (iterations 1-1000)
steps.step4: Compare: ALBP stability vs AB-sampler stability
steps.step5: Success criterion: AB-sampler shows >2× higher variance than ALBP in early phase
steps.step6: Visualize: Parameter trajectories for different initializations
steps.step7: Seeds: 10 seeds per strategy - PARALLEL across 2 cores

title: Phase 3c: Multimodality Challenge
description: Evaluate cyclical scheduling advantage on multimodal distributions

steps.step1: Generate multimodal targets: Mixture of discrete Gaussians, 2-6 modes (reduced)
steps.step2: Dimension: 100D, modes separated by Hamming distance ~50
steps.step3: Methods: ALBP, AB-sampler (fixed targets) vs ACS (cyclical)
steps.step4: Measure: Number of modes discovered (via clustering samples)
steps.step5: Measure: Mode-switching rate
steps.step6: Seeds: 10 seeds per (num_modes, method) - PARALLEL across 2 cores
steps.step7: Expected: ACS discovers ≥2× more modes than fixed-target methods

title: Phase 4: CPU Wall-Clock Benchmark
description: Practical CPU-focused benchmark using same instances as DISCS for direct comparability
priority: Cuttable (if over budget, skip this step)

steps.step1: DISCS instances: Lattice Ising 400D, Random Ising 400D (exact same problems)
steps.step2: Scaling: Test dimensions 100, 400, 900 variables
steps.step3: Methods: ALBP, AB-sampler, Fixed-step (reduced from 5 to 3 methods)
steps.step4: Target: ESS = 100 (adequate for inference)
steps.step5: Measure: Total wall-clock time = adaptation_time + sampling_to_target_ESS
steps.step6: Seeds: 3 seeds per (dimension, method) - PARALLEL across 2 cores
steps.step8: Analysis: Where is CPU viable vs GPU necessary? Compare against DISCS GPU times

title: Ablation: Adaptation Schedule Sensitivity
description: Ablate the effect of adaptation hyperparameters on performance

steps.step1: ALBP ablation: Vary learning rate η_0 ∈ {0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2}
steps.step2: AB-sampler ablation: Vary window size W ∈ {50, 100, 200}
steps.step3: ACS ablation: Vary cycle length T_i ∈ {500, 1000, 2000}
steps.step4: Problem: 400D Lattice Ising (J=0.2) for all ablations
steps.step5: Seeds: 5 seeds per configuration
steps.step6: Measure: Final ESS and adaptation convergence speed
steps.step7: Output: ablation_results.json showing sensitivity to hyperparameters

title: Ablation: Balancing Function Type
description: Test interaction between adaptation criterion and balancing function

steps.step1: Balancing functions: Barker (√t/(1+√t)), Tanh (tanh(t/2)),Sqrt(√t)
steps.step2: Methods: ALBP (assumes Barker) vs AB-sampler (adapts balancing)
steps.step3: Problem: 200D Lattice Ising at J=0.2 and J=0.44
steps.step4: Seeds: 5 seeds per (balancing, method, temperature)
steps.step5: Measure: ESS for each combination
steps.step6: Output: balancing_ablation.json

title: Evaluate Success Criteria and Mixed Results
description: Assess all success criteria and characterize any mixed results patterns

steps.step1: Criterion 1: ALBP achieves ≥80% optimal ESS on ≥70% of problems with <20% CV
steps.step2: Criterion 2: AB-sampler shows >2× higher early-phase variance than ALBP
steps.step3: Criterion 3: Both methods show >50% degradation at critical temperature
steps.step4: Criterion 4: ACS discovers ≥2× more modes on multimodal targets
steps.step5: Mixed results analysis: If methods win on different problem classes, characterize pattern
steps.step6: Classification: Build decision tree mapping problem characteristics → best method
steps.step7: Statistical summary: Report which criteria confirmed, which refuted, which mixed
steps.step8: Output: success_evaluation.json with binary and nuanced assessments

title: Robustness Analysis Across Random Seeds
description: Quantify method robustness via coefficient of variation across seeds

steps.step1: For each (problem, method), compute mean and std of ESS across 10 seeds
steps.step2: Compute coefficient of variation: CV = std/mean
steps.step3: Rank methods by CV per problem class (lower = more robust)
steps.step4: Test: Is ALBP significantly more robust than AB-sampler? (F-test for variance)
steps.step5: Visualize: Box plots of ESS distributions across methods
steps.step6: Output: robustness_analysis.json

title: Generate Paper Figures
description: Create publication-quality figures for results presentation

steps.step1: Figure 1: Method comparison bar chart with error bars (ESS by method/problem)
steps.step2: Figure 2: Phase transition plot showing degradation at J=0.44
steps.step3: Figure 3: Adaptation trajectories showing ALBP stability vs AB-sampler variance
steps.step4: Figure 4: Multimodality results - mode discovery counts by method
steps.step5: Figure 5: CPU benchmark scaling curves (time vs dimension)
steps.step6: Figure 6: Effect size heatmap showing pairwise method comparisons
steps.step7: Table 1: Summary statistics (mean ESS, CV, acceptance rate) by method
steps.step8: Table 2: Decision tree for method selection
steps.step9: Save all to figures/ in PDF and PNG formats

title: Generate Final Report and Documentation
description: Compile complete experimental report with all results

steps.step1: Generate summary report: results/summary_report.md
steps.step2: Include: Experimental setup, all results, statistical analysis, conclusions
steps.step3: Document: Which success criteria were met, which were not, which were mixed
steps.step4: Create code documentation: README with usage instructions
steps.step5: Package reproducibility: requirements.txt, run_all.sh script
steps.step6: Log all execution: Copy logs/ to results/execution_logs/
